stimulus: As a developmental psychologist, Alison Gopnik of the University of California, Berkeley, argues that young children reason about the world in surprisingly systematic ways. Gopnik’s work can therefore challenge classical cognitive ______ that children cannot grasp abstract causal structure—routinely overlook.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. The dashes after "theories" and "structure" correctly enclose the supplementary element "which insist…structure," indicating that this element could be removed without affecting the grammatical structure of the sentence.

Choice A is incorrect because it fails to use any punctuation to mark the supplementary element. Choice B is incorrect because it places the opening dash after "which," interrupting the supplementary clause at an illogical and grammatically incoherent point. Choice C is incorrect because a comma cannot be paired with a dash to enclose a supplementary element; punctuation marks used to bracket a parenthetical must match.
